NASDAQ END-OF-WEEK STOCK REPORT

www.buysellsignals.com

Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ:EXC $45.02), the NASDAQ's 4th largest Utility company by market cap, has dropped $2 (or 4.3%) in the past week to close at $45.02.

This makes it the sixth lowest performer in the NASDAQ-100 Index which was up 5.2% in the past week; a relative price change of -9.4%.

During the week its volatility (highest price minus lowest price/lowest price) of 6.0% was 2 times its average weekly volatility of 3.9%.

In the past week the market cap has declined by $2 billion to $45.5 billion.

Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ:EXC) operates as a leading energy provider, focusing on sustainable energy investments and grid modernization. With its strategic emphasis on renewable energy integration and the development of smart grid solutions, Exelon is advancing its vehicle-to-grid (V2G) pilot program and virtual power plant (VPP) technology. Key initiatives include a substantial commitment to capital expenditure targets, supporting ComEd's anticipated 19 GW load increase by 2030 in Illinois. Exelon's comprehensive approach to electric vehicle load growth and distributed energy resources (DER) underscores its commitment to enhancing energy transition strategies and regulatory reform to manage energy costs effectively.

Financial News

Annual Report (FY 2025):

Release Date: February 13, 2026

FY2025 Annual Report: Exelon Net Profit up 13%

Exelon (NASDAQ:EXC) reported net profit for the year-ended 31 December 2025 [FY2025] of $2.8b, up 13% from $2.5b in the previous year [FY2024]. Earnings per share (EPS) were up 12% from $2.45 in FY2024 to $2.74 in FY2025.

Annual growth in Revenue, Net Profit and EPS

BEARISH BREAKOUT

A bearish breakout is evident in the stock as price weakens on volume which is 1.3 times its average volume, after breaking past a defined support level of $45.

Wednesday's price drop

Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ:EXC) drops to three-month low on firm volume

CHICAGO, Wednesday, May 06 2026 - Exelon Corporation's (NASDAQ: EXC $45.01) stock price traded at its 83-day low of $45.0. The last time the price hit that low level was on Thursday, Feb 12, 2026. Exelon Corporation's stock price fell $1.17 (2.5%) from Tuesday's close of $45.01. Compared with the NASDAQ-100 Index which rose 584.1 points (2.1%) in the day, the relative price change was -4.6%. There were 9,225,462 shares worth $415.2 million traded today. The fall was exacerbated by firm volume of 1.3 times the average daily volume of 7.2 million shares.

SECTION 6 CORPORATE PROFILE

6.1 Activities

Exelon Corporation is a prominent U.S.-based energy company listed on the Nasdaq, specializing in the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity, as well as natural gas delivery. As one of the largest utilities in the nation, it operates through subsidiaries like Commonwealth Edison, PECO, and BGE, serving millions of customers across multiple states. The company's core activities encompass a diverse energy portfolio, including nuclear, fossil fuel, and renewable sources, with a strong emphasis on reliable and sustainable power supply. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, particularly wind and solar projects, driven by increasing demand for clean energy and supportive regulatory environments. Exelon is also advancing grid modernization and smart technology investments to enhance efficiency and integrate more renewables, positioning it as a key player in the transition to a low-carbon future while maintaining operational excellence and financial stability. It is the NASDAQ's 4th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Exelon Corporation, NASDAQ:EXC>

Exelon Corporation's capex plan is strategically crafted, encompassing the EXC capex guidance and a forward-looking Exelon capital expenditure plan. The Exelon 10-year capex plan is an integral part of its capital deployment strategy, emphasizing Exelon rate base growth and infrastructure investment. A focus on Exelon grid modernization projects and smart grid upgrades marks a significant stride in enhancing energy delivery. Key projects include Exelon transmission projects and distribution upgrades, underlining a strong commitment to regulated utility capex. The Exelon nuclear fleet investment along with renewable energy investments affirms the company’s dedication to a sustainable future. Notably, Exelon's battery storage projects further reinforce this commitment, supported by clean energy transition capex. Maintenance and growth capex are pivotal as the capex targets evolve. The company’s capex strategy is outlined in the Exelon investor presentation, impacting both earnings and dividend outlook. As the Exelon stock EXC garners attention, its target price reflects dynamic shifts like rate case impact and capital allocation priorities. The inclusion of Exelon ESG and decarbonization targets highlights its aligned infrastructure spending forecast. Evidently, the story is shifting for Exelon, with capital spending cresting towards 2026.

urn:contentItem:6JHR-KV33-S2KX-K4PD-00000-00Quarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 28 March 2026: Littelfuse reports recovery in Net Profit2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:48ZLexisNexisQuarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 28 March 2026: Littelfuse reports recovery in Net Profit

AMERICAN RESULTS

Littelfuse (NASDAQ:LFUS) announced net profit for the quarter-ended 28 March 2026 [Q1/2026] of $75.1m, recovered from $242m in the previous quarter [Q4/2025].

Major changes compared with previous corresponding period [Mar 28, 2026 vs Mar 29, 2025]:

Favourable Changes:

- Net profit was up 72.5% from $43.6m to $75.1m

- Total revenue was up 17.1% from $559.1m to $654.6m

- EPS growth 70.5% from $1.76 to $3.0

- EBITDA to Sales was up from 14.9% to 18.4%

- EBIT to Sales was up from 12.3% to 15.8%

- Profit before tax to Sales was up from 10.7% to 14.8%

ACTIVITIES:

Littelfuse, Inc., a global leader in circuit protection technologies, has been providing innovative solutions since its founding in 1927. Headquartered in Chicago, Illinois, the company designs, manufactures, and markets a diverse range of products, including fuses, semiconductors, sensors, and protection relays, primarily serving the automotive, industrial, and electronics sectors. Its core business revolves around safeguarding electrical systems from overcurrent, overvoltage, and other hazards, enhancing safety and reliability across applications. The fastest-growing segments include automotive electronics, fueled by the rising adoption of electric vehicles and advanced driver-assistance systems, as well as industrial automation and renewable energy solutions, where demand for efficient protection components is surging. With a strong international footprint, Littelfuse continues to invest in R&D to address evolving market demands and maintain its competitive edge.

MARKET POSITION:

Littelfuse, Inc. (NASDAQ:LFUS), the NASDAQ's 5th largest Electronics company by market cap, soared $189.31 (or 75%) year-to-date (YTD) in 2026 to close at $441.69. Compared with the NASDAQ-100 Index which has risen 13.3% YTD, this is a relative price increase of 61.7%. A zero-bagger in 2025, the value of USD1,000 invested two years ago is $1,868 [vs $1,594 for the NASDAQ-100 Index], for a capital gain of $826 and dividend reinvested of $42. The average annualised return to shareholders has been 36.7% in the past two years. The average daily volume of 236,584 shares so far in 2026 was 1.2 times the average daily volume in 2025.

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHH-G7C3-SFSX-M17P-00000-00CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC. - THE RECENT ON INDUSTRY AND PEER DEVELOPMENTS2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:10ZLexisNexisSTOCKCLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC. - THE RECENT ON INDUSTRY AND PEER DEVELOPMENTS

DASHBOARD: CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY

PermID4296495921Websitehttp://www.claycorp.comIndustryConstructionAddress2199 Innerbelt Business Center Drive Overland, Missouri 63114

ACTIVITIES:

Clayco Construction Company, Inc., founded in 1984 by Bob Clark, is a design-build firm headquartered in St. Louis, Missouri. With over 3,800 employees and $7.6 billion in 2024 revenue, it serves commercial, industrial, and life sciences sectors. Clayco offers architecture, engineering, and construction services through subsidiaries like Lamar Johnson Collaborative and Concrete Strategies. Known for projects like the Novus Headquarters, it emphasizes sustainable, LEED-certified buildings. In 2024, Clayco expanded into renewable energy and data center construction. Its culture fosters innovation, safety, and diversity, with robust training programs. Competing with DPR Construction, Clayco stands out for its integrated approach and fast-track delivery. Recent initiatives include advancing technology-driven construction and eco-friendly practices.

+ CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY [3 News That Matters]

+ CLAYCO UNVEILS POWER AND ENERGY UNIT AIMING FOR 1,000 JOBS BY 2027

Chicago-based contractor Clayco has formed a new division dedicated to large solar and storage projects across the country. Unveiled on 4th March 2026, the unit, Clayco Power and Energy, targets almost 1,000 U.S. jobs by 2027 and expects roughly $300 million in annual revenue.Clayco Power and Energy will provide design, engineering, construction, and procurement for solar and battery storage systems. It is currently developing a 350-acre solar-plus-storage project in Manteno, Illinois, about an hour south of Chicago, with construction due to start this June.The move aligns with a projected rise in electricity demand of 15%–20% by 2030, driven by data centers, manufacturing, and the shift to electrified transportation. Notably, last year saw solar plus storage accounting for the majority of new capacity, underscoring the market tailwinds behind solar deployment.

+ CLAYCO PARTNERS WITH DEEP ATOMIC ON U.S. NUCLEAR-POWERED AI DATA CENTER AT IDAHO NATIONAL LABORATORY

Clayco, a leading design-build firm specializing in complex mission-critical facilities, has announced its collaboration with Swiss-American energy company Deep Atomic to support submissions to the U.S. Department of Energy for a nuclear-powered AI data center campus planned at Idaho National Laboratory, a project that could usher in a new era of energy-efficient computing.On 12th February 2026, Clayco said it would provide early-stage delivery planning, constructability analysis, and integrated design-build expertise as part of the consortium. The firm’s track record in delivering large-scale data centers positions it to help ensure the campus can be designed and built safely, efficiently, and at scale. Bob Clark, Executive Chairman and Founder of Clayco, stressed that confidence in project delivery is essential for a successful DOE submission.Deep Atomic’s approach blends advanced nuclear energy with AI data center infrastructure to deliver reliable, low-carbon power for next-generation computing. The partnership aims to establish a scalable, sustainable platform that could position the United States as a leader in clean-energy AI infrastructure; Deep Atomic founder and CEO William G. J. Theron said the mission is to accelerate deployment of advanced nuclear power to meet performance and sustainability needs, with Clayco guiding execution planning for the DOE submission.

+ CLARK URGES UNIFIED ACTION TO REBUILD FOUNTAIN PARK AFTER MAY 16 TORNADO

ST. LOUIS, Mo. (First Alert 4) - Nearly a year after the May 16 tornado devastated North St. Louis, Clayco CEO Bob Clark told business and civic leaders that rallying with political figures is essential to craft a unified reconstruction strategy for areas already under stress. Standing in Fountain Park, he said the recovery has been slow but the neighborhood shows potential for renewal.Clark said Clayco moved quickly after the storm, coordinating with Mayor Cara Spencer and city services along with local partners. The company’s long-term rebuilding effort includes funding and staffing community programs with Albion and Washington University to improve quality of life and keep the city moving forward. He praised residents for supporting each other in the immediate aftermath in the absence of formal plans.Looking ahead, he described Fountain Park as a beacon of hope and noted developments that were already under way before the tornado, including city programs such as the St. Louis Development Corporation and the Land Clearance for Redevelopment Authority that promote new housing and help families find safe, affordable homes. He stressed that everything starts with the civic community and a shared will to navigate recovery.Clark framed recovery as a gradual process, drawing on parallels to Hurricane Katrina and underscoring the need to start somewhere. He reiterated a vision of incremental progress: one house at a time, one block at a time, one brick at a time.Despite challenges, including more than 65% of affected property owners not applying for city programs, the city extended the deadline for its private property assistance program to February 28 to boost participation. Clark pointed to visible improvements since the tornado and cited the Delmar Divine development as evidence that teamwork can drive meaningful change.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ RYAN COMPANIES [2 News That Matters]

+ RYAN TO BOLSTER PROPERTY TAX CAPABILITIES WITH MERITAX ADVISORS BUY

Ryan Companies has announced the acquisition of Meritax Advisors, a Texas-based commercial property tax consulting firm, with the move made official on the 10th of February 2026. The deal broadens Ryan's property tax capabilities for commercial real estate owners amid a more technology-driven landscape, emphasizing enhancements in valuation strategy, litigation management and large-portfolio advisory services.Meritax Advisors, founded in 1995, has earned a strong reputation for property tax representation across varied commercial real estate sectors and manages tens of billions of dollars in assessed value for institutional and private clients in Texas and beyond. The integration is designed to augment Ryan's existing property tax services by bringing deeper analytics, real-time intelligence and predictive insights to support technology-driven advocacy for clients.Damon Chronis, President of U.S. Operations at Ryan, stated, 'With Meritax Advisors joining Ryan, we’re adding a disciplined, client-focused team with deep knowledge of the Texas property tax landscape.' The firm notes that the acquisition will strengthen Ryan's ability to serve commercial real estate owners not only in Texas but also on a national scale, leveraging Ryan's global footprint and proprietary technology.As part of the integration, key Meritax leaders will join Ryan, including Ryan Chismark, Charlie Honea and Mark Kline, along with 20 additional team members. The combined organization underscores Ryan’s commitment to expanding its Property Tax practice in a competitive market and aligns with the broader goal of serving more than 74,000 clients worldwide with a 7,100-strong professional team.

+ RYAN COMPANIES' ANNA GRIFFIN APPLIES AIR FORCE EXPERIENCE TO SOUTHWEST CONSTRUCTION PROJECTS

In a new segment of the AZ Big Podcast, Anna Griffin, who oversees construction operations for Ryan Companies US, discussed how leadership and resilience are informed by her military career. The interview was recorded 5 days ago and aligns with Veterans Day by featuring Griffin, who moved from military service to directing construction projects across the Southwest. Griffin had recently returned from a seven-month deployment to Jordan, where she served as aircraft maintenance squadron commander for the 165th Airlift Wing. She observed that "Construction has so many parallels to my military service," adding, "You’re still serving — just in a different way. You’re building something lasting, something that supports people and communities." At Ryan Companies, she directs project management teams across healthcare, multifamily housing, and education, and, after nearly four years with the firm, her leadership helps maneuver the intricacies of construction sites. She underscores integrity, accountability, and mission readiness as enduring traits from her military days that inform civilian projects. The AZ Big Podcast, hosted by Michael Gossie and Amy Lindsey, explores notable stories in Arizona's business scene. Sponsored by Burch & Cracchiolo, a prominent law firm, the show seeks to illustrate how effective leadership and collaboration propel results across industries. Griffin's appearance arrives at a moment when Arizona honors its veterans and recognizes the marketable skills they bring to the workforce. Listeners are invited to hear Griffin's compelling narrative and learn how veterans like her contribute meaningfully to fields such as construction. As she continues her role with Ryan Companies, her path illustrates the resilience and adaptability of veterans transitioning to new responsibilities, reinforcing that military-honed skills remain valuable across sectors.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: Silgan Closures Invests In Cleaner Energy In Illinois

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH6-BGJ3-S2S7-P476-00000-00Silgan Closures Invests In Cleaner Energy In Illinois2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:48ZLexisNexisSilgan Closures Invests In Cleaner Energy In Illinois

Global manufacturer invests to achieve Illinois' renewable energy requirements
DOWNERS GROVE, Ill., May 5, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Silgan Closures, a global leader in food and beverage closure manufacturing with headquarters in Illinois, is joining statewide efforts to expand energy reliability and sustainability by investing in community wind and solar projects. The Company's goal is to offset 75 percent of its electricity consumption with clean energy credits within the next five years and to reduce its own energy use at two manufacturing facilities in Illinois.


Illinois legislation presently calls for all electricity in the state to come from renewable sources and nuclear power by 2050. To help meet this goal, Silgan Closures has partnered with leading renewable energy developers to contractually support wind and solar electricity generation projects in Illinois.
"At Silgan Closures, our roots go back nearly a century to a family-owned Illinois business. Today, as a global leader in food and beverage closure manufacturing, we remain deeply embedded in the communities we serve," said Silgan Closures President Liz Wiese. "Our latest investments in cleaner energy here in Illinois reflect that commitment – not only to our customers, but to driving sustainable economic growth where our employees live, work, and rely on our products in their homes."
Additionally, through its partnership with the U.S. Department of Energy's Better Plants Program, Silgan Closures is taking steps to increase energy efficiency at its facilities in Illinois, which is expected to result in reduced energy consumption at these plants.
About Silgan Closures:
Silgan Closures is a global supplier of plastic, metal, and composite closure systems for food and beverage products. The Silgan Closures U.S. headquarters and innovation center are located in Downers Grove, Illinois. The European headquarters are located in Munich, Germany with global operations across five continents. For more information, visit the company website at or contact Theadora Iacobellis at [email protected].
SOURCE Silgan Closures

NASDAQ END-OF-WEEK STOCK REPORT

www.buysellsignals.com

Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ:EXC $46.18), the NASDAQ's 4th largest Utility company by market cap, has ticked lower by 86c (or 1.8%) in the past week to close at $46.18. Compared with the NASDAQ-100 Index which rose 986.0 points (or 3.6%) in the week, this represented a relative price change of -5.5%. The volume was 2 times average weekly trading of 35.9 million shares.

In the past week the market cap has declined by $868.6 million to $46.6 billion.

Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ:EXC) operates as a leading energy provider, focusing on sustainable energy investments and grid modernization. With its strategic emphasis on renewable energy integration and the development of smart grid solutions, Exelon is advancing its vehicle-to-grid (V2G) pilot program and virtual power plant (VPP) technology. Key initiatives include a substantial commitment to capital expenditure targets, supporting ComEd's anticipated 19 GW load increase by 2030 in Illinois. Exelon's comprehensive approach to electric vehicle load growth and distributed energy resources (DER) underscores its commitment to enhancing energy transition strategies and regulatory reform to manage energy costs effectively.

Financial News

Annual Report (FY 2025):

Release Date: February 13, 2026

FY2025 Annual Report: Exelon Net Profit up 13%

Exelon (NASDAQ:EXC) reported net profit for the year-ended 31 December 2025 [FY2025] of $2.8b, up 13% from $2.5b in the previous year [FY2024]. Earnings per share (EPS) were up 12% from $2.45 in FY2024 to $2.74 in FY2025.

SECTION 6 CORPORATE PROFILE

6.1 Activities

Exelon Corporation is a prominent U.S.-based energy company listed on the Nasdaq, specializing in the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity, as well as natural gas delivery. As one of the largest utilities in the nation, it operates through subsidiaries like Commonwealth Edison, PECO, and BGE, serving millions of customers across multiple states. The company's core activities encompass a diverse energy portfolio, including nuclear, fossil fuel, and renewable sources, with a strong emphasis on reliable and sustainable power supply. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, particularly wind and solar projects, driven by increasing demand for clean energy and supportive regulatory environments. Exelon is also advancing grid modernization and smart technology investments to enhance efficiency and integrate more renewables, positioning it as a key player in the transition to a low-carbon future while maintaining operational excellence and financial stability. It is the NASDAQ's 4th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Exelon Corporation, NASDAQ:EXC>

Exelon Corporation's capex plan is strategically crafted, encompassing the EXC capex guidance and a forward-looking Exelon capital expenditure plan. The Exelon 10-year capex plan is an integral part of its capital deployment strategy, emphasizing Exelon rate base growth and infrastructure investment. A focus on Exelon grid modernization projects and smart grid upgrades marks a significant stride in enhancing energy delivery. Key projects include Exelon transmission projects and distribution upgrades, underlining a strong commitment to regulated utility capex. The Exelon nuclear fleet investment along with renewable energy investments affirms the company’s dedication to a sustainable future. Notably, Exelon's battery storage projects further reinforce this commitment, supported by clean energy transition capex. Maintenance and growth capex are pivotal as the capex targets evolve. The company’s capex strategy is outlined in the Exelon investor presentation, impacting both earnings and dividend outlook. As the Exelon stock EXC garners attention, its target price reflects dynamic shifts like rate case impact and capital allocation priorities. The inclusion of Exelon ESG and decarbonization targets highlights its aligned infrastructure spending forecast. Evidently, the story is shifting for Exelon, with capital spending cresting towards 2026.

urn:contentItem:6JH4-BNG3-S9G3-61M0-00000-00CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC. - RECENT DEVELOPMENTS: RYAN COMPANIES AND PEERS2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:10ZLexisNexisSTOCKCLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC. - RECENT DEVELOPMENTS: RYAN COMPANIES AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY

PermID4296495921Websitehttp://www.claycorp.comIndustryConstructionAddress2199 Innerbelt Business Center Drive Overland, Missouri 63114

ACTIVITIES:

Clayco Construction Company, Inc., founded in 1984 by Bob Clark, is a design-build firm headquartered in St. Louis, Missouri. With over 3,800 employees and $7.6 billion in 2024 revenue, it serves commercial, industrial, and life sciences sectors. Clayco offers architecture, engineering, and construction services through subsidiaries like Lamar Johnson Collaborative and Concrete Strategies. Known for projects like the Novus Headquarters, it emphasizes sustainable, LEED-certified buildings. In 2024, Clayco expanded into renewable energy and data center construction. Its culture fosters innovation, safety, and diversity, with robust training programs. Competing with DPR Construction, Clayco stands out for its integrated approach and fast-track delivery. Recent initiatives include advancing technology-driven construction and eco-friendly practices.

+ CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY [3 News That Matters]

+ CHICAGO BUILDER CLAYCO BETS ON SOLAR AND STORAGE WITH NEW NATIONAL ARM

Clayco, a Chicago-based contractor, is expanding into solar and storage with a new national division, announced on 4th March 2026. The venture, known as Clayco Power and Energy, aims to create nearly 1,000 jobs nationwide by 2027 and generate about $300 million in annual revenue.The unit will oversee design, engineering, construction, and procurement for solar and storage projects and is already advancing a 350-acre Manteno, Illinois site, with construction slated to begin this June.The expansion comes as U.S. electricity demand is forecast to rise 15%–20% by 2030, with solar and storage contributing a growing share of new capacity after accounting for roughly 85% of last year’s additions.

+ IDAHO NATIONAL LAB TO HOST NUCLEAR-POWERED AI DATA CENTER: CLAYCO TO LEAD DOE PROPOSAL DESIGN-BUILD EFFORT

Clayco, a leading design-build firm specializing in complex mission-critical facilities, has announced its collaboration with Swiss-American energy company Deep Atomic to support submissions to the U.S. Department of Energy for a nuclear-powered AI data center campus planned at Idaho National Laboratory, a project that could usher in a new era of energy-efficient computing.On 12th February 2026, Clayco said it would provide early-stage delivery planning, constructability analysis, and integrated design-build expertise as part of the consortium. The firm’s track record in delivering large-scale data centers positions it to help ensure the campus can be designed and built safely, efficiently, and at scale. Bob Clark, Executive Chairman and Founder of Clayco, stressed that confidence in project delivery is essential for a successful DOE submission.In its advisory role, Clayco will align design and construction approaches with the operational requirements of high-density AI workloads, leveraging its integrated delivery model to meet the fast, reliable, and precise needs of modern data centers. The consortium brings together organizations with expertise in advanced nuclear technology, energy systems, and risk modeling, complementing Clayco's practical delivery capabilities. Deep Atomic's approach combines advanced nuclear energy with AI data center infrastructure to ensure reliable, low-carbon power for next-generation computing. The collaboration aims to establish a scalable platform that could position the U.S. as a leader in clean-energy AI infrastructure; Deep Atomic founder and CEO William G. J. Theron said the mission is to accelerate deployment of advanced nuclear power to meet performance and sustainability needs, with Clayco helping integrate innovative energy solutions and execution planning for the DOE submission.

+ FOUNTAIN PARK AS BEACON OF RENEWAL: CLAYCO'S CLARK CALLS FOR COLLABORATIVE RECOVERY

Nearly a year after the May 16 tornado devastated North St. Louis, Clayco CEO Bob Clark says the business community must join with city leaders to forge a unified rebuilding strategy for areas that faced long-standing struggles. In Fountain Park, he voiced cautious optimism about renewal and noted the visible damage from the disaster.Clark said Clayco acted promptly after the storm, coordinating with Mayor Spencer, city services, and local partners. The company’s commitment to long-term rebuilding includes funding and staffing programs with Albion and Washington University to improve quality of life and keep the city moving forward, complemented by ongoing community support.Describing Fountain Park as a potential beacon of hope, Clark highlighted preexisting programs that promote new housing and help families find safe, affordable homes, such as the St. Louis Development Corporation and the Land Clearance for Redevelopment Authority. He emphasized that real recovery starts with the civic community and requires a shared will to move forward, adding that progress happens incrementally—one house at a time, one block at a time.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ RYAN COMPANIES [2 News That Matters]

+ MERITAX ADVISORS JOINS RYAN COMPANIES TO ACCELERATE PROPERTY TAX SOLUTIONS

Ryan Companies expanded its property tax services with the acquisition of Meritax Advisors, announced official on the 10th of February 2026. Meritax, a Texas-based commercial property tax consultant, has supported clients across various real estate sectors and manages tens of billions of dollars in assessed value in Texas and beyond.The deal is designed to boost Ryan's capabilities in valuation strategy, litigation management and large-portfolio advisory services, while incorporating Meritax's analytics-driven approach and technology-enabled advocacy to keep pace with evolving property tax systems. Damon Chronis, President of U.S. Operations at Ryan, said the addition brings a disciplined, client-focused team with deep Texas knowledge to the firm.As part of the integration, Meritax partners including Ryan Chismark, Charlie Honea and Mark Kline, along with about 20 other team members, will join Ryan. The move adds to Ryan’s global footprint, which already serves more than 74,000 clients with a workforce of over 7,100 professionals, reinforcing the firm’s leading position in business taxes.

+ VETERANS DAY FEATURE: ANNA GRIFFIN CONNECTS MILITARY SERVICE TO CONSTRUCTION EXCELLENCE AT RYAN

Anna Griffin, director of construction for Ryan Companies US, sat down on the AZ Big Podcast to discuss how her military background informs leadership and resilience in the construction industry. The interview, recorded 5 days ago, comes as Veterans Day is observed in Arizona and highlights Griffin's trajectory from Air Force service to overseeing Southwest construction projects. Griffin recently returned from a seven-month deployment to Jordan as aircraft maintenance squadron commander for the 165th Airlift Wing, and she notes that the two fields share common ground: "Construction has so many parallels to my military service," she noted. "You’re still serving — just in a different way. You’re building something lasting, something that supports people and communities." At Ryan Companies, Griffin guides project management teams across healthcare, multifamily housing, and education. After nearly four years at the firm, her leadership helps navigate the complexity of construction environments, with a focus on integrity, accountability, and mission readiness, qualities that served her in the military and now guide civilian projects. The AZ Big Podcast, hosted by Michael Gossie and Amy Lindsey, is sponsored by Burch & Cracchiolo and aims to spotlight how strong leadership and collaboration drive success across industries in Arizona's business landscape. Griffin's episode is timely as the state honors its veterans and recognizes the valuable skills they contribute to the workforce. Listeners are encouraged to tune in to hear her compelling story and understand how veterans like her are making meaningful contributions to sectors such as construction. As she continues her work with Ryan Companies, her journey demonstrates the strength and adaptability of veterans transitioning into fresh roles, underscoring that the capabilities developed in military service are transferable across fields.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JH0-60M3-RWHH-2349-00000-00DTE Energy's dividend in perspective: comparison with Exelon Corporation *Updated*2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:48ZLexisNexisDTE Energy's dividend in perspective: comparison with Exelon Corporation *Updated*

NYSE STOCK REPORT

DTE Energy's trailing twelve months dividend is $4.51. Its dividend yield at its current price is 2.97%. Its current price is at a 1.1% discount to its 52 week high. Its current price is at a 21.1% premium to its 52 week low. If DTE Energy was to return to its 52 week low its dividend yield would be 3.6%.

In comparison, Exelon Corporation's trailing twelve months dividend is $1.62. Its dividend yield at its current price is 3.52%. Its current price is at a 9.2% discount to its 52 week high. Its current price is at a 13.2% premium to its 52 week low. If Exelon Corporation was to return to its 52 week low its dividend yield would be 3.99%.

$1000 invested into DTE Energy stock 5 years ago would be worth $1,679 today. This total gain of $679 is generated by a capital gain of $456 and dividends of $223.

$1000 invested into Exelon Corporation stock 5 years ago would be worth $2,020 today. This total gain of $1,020 is generated by a capital gain of $713 and dividends of $307.

DTE Energy's dividend has grown 7.1% over the last 4 years.

Exelon Corporation's dividend has grown 5.7% over the last 4 years.

Company Profile

DTE Energy, headquartered in Detroit, operates as a diversified energy company with its primary focus on regulated electric and natural gas utilities. The company serves 2.3 million electric customers in Southeast Michigan and 1.4 million gas customers statewide, making these utilities its largest revenue sources. Its portfolio also includes non-utility businesses specializing in custom energy solutions, renewable energy generation, and energy marketing and trading. The fastest-growing segments include clean energy development, such as solar parks under the MIGreenPower program, and hyperscale data center contracts like the 1.4 GW Oracle agreement. These initiatives drive economic progress, affordability benefits, and advancements toward net-zero goals.

Company Profile

Exelon Corporation operates as a holding company for regulated electric and gas utilities, primarily generating revenue through electricity transmission and distribution, which accounts for approximately 85% of total revenue. The largest revenue contributors are its PHI subsidiary, focused on Mid-Atlantic operations, and ComEd, serving northern Illinois, which together represent over 58% of the company's $24.3 billion full-year revenue. The fastest-growing segments include BGE, driven by Maryland distribution rates, and PECO, supported by Pennsylvania electric and gas rates and favorable weather conditions. These segments have shown significant year-over-year growth, reflecting the company's investments in infrastructure and regulatory recoveries.

value.Title: HD Hyundai Electric wins U.S. Midwest transformer deal
value.Overview: Azernews - News from Azerbaijan, Business, Energy, Analysis, HYUNDAI MOTOR COMPANY NEWS, HYUNDAI MOTOR COMPANY NEWS & US NEWS, 311words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHM-FV43-RX4D-P3GF-00000-00HD Hyundai Electric wins U.S. Midwest transformer deal2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:48ZLexisNexisHYUNDAI MOTOR COMPANY NEWS, HYUNDAI MOTOR COMPANY NEWS & US NEWSHD Hyundai Electric wins U.S. Midwest transformer deal

By Alimat Aliyeva

HD Hyundai Electric has secured a $120 million contract to
supply 765-kilovolt ultra-high-voltage transformers and reactors to
a major utility company in the U.S. Midwest,
AzerNEWS reports, citing Korean media.

The deal places the company within the 765-kV backbone segment
of the Southwest Power Pool (SPP) long-term transmission master
plan, a key initiative aimed at strengthening and expanding
transmission infrastructure across the south-central U.S. power
grid.

The SPP region, which hosts one of the densest wind energy
clusters in the United States, is becoming a critical area where
rapidly growing renewable generation is meeting the urgent need for
high-capacity grid modernization. As wind and solar output
increase, utilities are under pressure to upgrade transmission
lines capable of moving large amounts of electricity over long
distances without congestion or losses.

The contract was finalized on the sidelines of the IEEE PES
T&D Conference & Exposition 2026, the largest power industry
exhibition in North America, held at McCormick Place in Chicago
from May 4 to 7. The biennial event attracted around 900 energy
companies this year, including global industry leaders such as ABB,
Siemens Energy, and Hitachi Energy.

At the event, HD Hyundai Electric also presented its 2030
technology roadmap, which includes sulfur hexafluoride (SF₆)-free
gas-insulated switchgear and UL-certified medium- and low-voltage
circuit breakers, reflecting a broader industry shift toward
reducing greenhouse gas emissions in electrical infrastructure.

765-kV transmission systems are among the highest voltage levels
used in commercial power grids worldwide. They are essential for
integrating large-scale renewable energy projects—especially wind
farms in the U.S. Midwest—into national grids, helping reduce
energy waste and improve long-distance electricity transfer
efficiency.

urn:contentItem:6JK2-7YM3-RRYG-S4F6-00000-00Richardson Electronics, Ltd. (NASDAQ:RELL) Forms Technology Partnership With Gotion to Deliver U.S.-Manufactured Battery Energy Storage Systems2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:48ZLexisNexisRichardson Electronics, Ltd. (NASDAQ:RELL) Forms Technology Partnership With Gotion to Deliver U.S.-Manufactured Battery Energy Storage Systems

AMERICAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

14 May 2026 01:11 EDT

LAFOX, Ill., May 13, 2026 — Richardson Electronics, Ltd. (NASDAQ:RELL) and Gotion Inc. forge a technology partnership to deliver large-scale battery energy storage systems to the U.S. market. The collaboration links Richardson Electronics’ power management and engineering capabilities in LaFox, Illinois with GotionIllinois’ Manteno battery manufacturing hub to serve commercial and industrial and utility-scale projects nationwide.

The joint BESS platforms include a 760-kWh system for C&I energy storage and a 5-MWh system for large-scale utility deployments, manufactured and assembled in Manteno, Illinois, to help customers manage rising electricity costs, optimize energy usage and strengthen grid resilience. The initiative aligns with Illinois Climate Resilience and Grid Advancement CRGA program, which offers rebates of up to 250 per kWh for standalone battery energy storage systems. Richardson Electronics will drive commercialization, engineering integration and go-to-market activities in the United States, leveraging its extensive U.S. market experience and global engineering support. Gotion will provide the advanced battery technology produced at its Manteno hub, reinforcing a localized supply chain for U.S. energy storage initiatives. This collaboration reflects Gotion’s commitment to building a localized, resilient U.S. battery supply chain, with both parties emphasizing scalable, domestically supported energy storage solutions for commercial, industrial and utility segments. The Illinois partnership aims to expand U.S.-based manufacturing and deployment of energy storage systems, supporting grid modernization and economic development. About Richardson Electronics, Ltd.Richardson Electronics, Ltd. is a global manufacturer of engineered solutions, green energy products, power grid and microwave tubes and related consumables; power conversion and RF and microwave components; CT X-ray tubes; and customized display solutions. More than 50% of its products are manufactured in LaFox, Illinois, Marlborough, MA, or Donaueschingen, Germany, or by partners worldwide. The company serves customers in alternative energy, healthcare, aviation, broadcast, communications, industrial, marine, medical, military, scientific and semiconductor markets. Richardson Electronics, Ltd. common stock trades on the NASDAQ Global Select Market under the ticker symbol RELL. Richardson Electronics Green Energy Solutions focuses on products for wind, solar, hydrogen and electric vehicles as well as other power management applications. For more information, visit rell.com. About Richardson Electronics Power & Microwave Technologies, For more than 75 years, Richardson Electronics has been a global provider of engineered solutions, RF & microwave and power products. The Power & Microwave Technologies group identifies and introduces disruptive technologies, develops global solutions and provides worldwide support. For more information, visit rellpower.com | relltubes.com | rellaser.com. About Gotion Inc.Gotion Inc. advances battery technology and energy storage solutions for North America, supporting localization of manufacturing and deployment across the United States. For more information, visit gotion.com. About Gotion Illinois Gotion Illinois develops advanced battery manufacturing capabilities in Manteno, Illinois, to support domestic battery energy storage systems for C&I and utility-scale markets. For more information, visit gotionillinois.com. Contact Information: Scott DuBois, Director Business Development | BESS Richardson Electronics Power & Microwave Technologies 630.277.2105 | sdubois@rell.com | rell.com Source: West Corporation

value.Title: April 16, 2026: Willamette University Graduates Drive Innovation in Solar Energy Policy

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK3-7J23-RTTV-W1T7-00000-00April 16, 2026: Willamette University Graduates Drive Innovation in Solar Energy Policy2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:48ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSApril 16, 2026: Willamette University Graduates Drive Innovation in Solar Energy Policy

Graduates from Willamette University are harnessing their education to shape the future of solar energy and climate policy in the United States. Notably, Conner Rettig BA’17, an English major who is now engaged in implementing solar policy in Illinois, remarked, "We’re on a solar coaster." The urgency of this transition is underscored by projections that solar panels will account for 80% of renewable energy growth through 2030. However, with changing federal policies and global economic dynamics, the stakes for renewable energy are higher than ever.

Willamette University alumni are actively engaged on the front lines of the renewable energy sector, leveraging their liberal arts education to adapt to the complexities of climate policy and solar technology. During a recent panel discussion organized by the Willamette University Sustainability Network, five alumni shared their experiences and insights on integrating their diverse academic backgrounds into impactful careers within the solar power industry. Their collective narrative highlights the university's motto: "Not unto ourselves alone are we born," inspiring graduates to make a meaningful difference in sustainability.

Panelist Marshall Curry BA’13, who studied Sociology along with minors in Chemistry and Spanish, emphasized the analytical skills he gained at Willamette University that allow him to dissect complex systems. His ability to understand the intricate components of issues like solar power has been instrumental in his work in international development and state government. Jessica Dusek MBA’13, now Project Development Manager at SolAmerica Energy, utilizes her MBA education to bridge renewable energy and agriculture, facilitating solar panel installations for farmers and landowners.

Rettig's approach to policy communication is another example of Willamette's influence, as he emphasizes the importance of storytelling to help communities grasp the significance of clean energy initiatives. Elizabeth Gill BA’17 has transitioned from federal government roles to the private sector, applying critical thinking skills learned from Willamette’s Politics, Policy, Law & Ethics program. Her focus on analytical inquiry has enabled her to navigate various renewable energy technologies effectively. Peter Wyrsch BA’12 employs his historical research skills to aid utilities in mitigating wildfire risks exacerbated by climate change.

Despite facing challenges in the solar energy sector, these Willamette University alumni are making significant contributions to combat climate change. The Pacific Northwest remains a pivotal area for renewable energy efforts, and the panelists agree on the region's capacity to drive progress in this field. Current students at Willamette are well-positioned to enter the renewable energy landscape, benefitting from interdisciplinary courses and hands-on research opportunities that cultivate the skills necessary to tackle complex climate issues. Gill noted that while technical expertise is valuable, graduates from Willamette can play crucial roles in facilitating project completion and collaboration within the energy sector.

INDEX

SECTION 1 WILLAMETTE UNIVERSITY PROFILE

SECTION 1 WILLAMETTE UNIVERSITY PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

Willamette University''s claim to fame is its status as the first university in the West. About 3,000 students are enrolled in the private, co-educational liberal arts school that offers undergraduate and graduate degrees. Undergraduate degrees encompass nearly 50 fields -- politics, biology, English, psychology, and economics are among the most pursued majors -- and graduate degrees in business, law, and education. The university has a student-to-faculty ratio of 10:1. Founded in the early days of the Oregon Territory by missionary Jason Lee as a school for Native American children, Willamette University was established in 1842.

1.2 SUMMARY

urn:contentItem:6JK2-7YM3-RRYG-S4CS-00000-00Richardson Electronics, Ltd. (NASDAQ:RELL) Partners With Gotion to Deliver U.S.-Manufactured Battery Energy Storage Systems2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:48ZLexisNexisRichardson Electronics, Ltd. (NASDAQ:RELL) Partners With Gotion to Deliver U.S.-Manufactured Battery Energy Storage Systems

AMERICAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

14 May 2026 01:15 EDT

LAFOX, Ill., May 13, 2026 — Richardson Electronics, Ltd. (NASDAQ:RELL) and Gotion Inc. announce a technology partnership to bring two large-scale BESS platforms to the U.S. market, expanding options for commercial, industrial and utility-scale deployments. The collaboration combines Richardson Electronics’ power management and engineering capabilities in LaFox, Illinois with GotionIllinois’ Manteno, Illinois manufacturing hub to support domestic energy storage programs.

The 760-kWh platform targets C&I installations while a 5-MWh system is designed for utility-scale projects, both manufactured and assembled in Manteno to support on-site energy management, cost control and improved grid resilience. The Illinois CRGA program offers rebates up to 250 per kWh for standalone BESS, strengthening project economics. Richardson Electronics will lead U.S. commercialization, engineering integration and go-to-market strategy, leveraging its market reach and technical support network. Gotion will supply the advanced battery technology produced at Manteno, enabling scalable, domestically supported energy storage solutions for commercial, industrial and utility segments. The partnership advances U.S.-based manufacturing and deployment of energy storage systems and aligns with broader efforts to modernize the grid and boost local economic development in Illinois and beyond. About Richardson Electronics, Ltd. Richardson Electronics, Ltd. is a global manufacturer of engineered solutions, green energy products, power grid and microwave tubes and related consumables; power conversion and RF and microwave components; CT X-ray tubes; and customized display solutions. More than 50% of its products are manufactured in LaFox, Illinois, Marlborough, MA, or Donaueschingen, Germany, or by partners worldwide. The company serves customers in alternative energy, healthcare, aviation, broadcast, communications, industrial, marine, medical, military, scientific and semiconductor markets. Richardson Electronics, Ltd. common stock trades on the NASDAQ Global Select Market under the ticker symbol RELL. Richardson Electronics Green Energy Solutions focuses on products for wind, solar, hydrogen and electric vehicles as well as other power management applications. For more information, visit rell.com. About Gotion Inc.Gotion Inc. advances battery technology and energy storage solutions for North America, supporting localization of manufacturing and deployment across the United States. For more information, visit gotion.com. About Gotion Illinois Gotion Illinois develops advanced battery manufacturing capabilities in Manteno, Illinois, to support domestic battery energy storage systems for C&I and utility-scale markets. For more information, visit gotionillinois.com. Contact Information: Scott DuBois, Director Business Development | BESS Richardson Electronics Power & Microwave Technologies 630.277.2105 | sdubois@rell.com | rell.com Source: West Corporation

NASDAQ END-OF-WEEK STOCK REPORT

www.buysellsignals.com

Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ:EXC $43.91), the NASDAQ's 4th largest Utility company by market cap, has fell $2.59 (or 5.6%) in the past week to close at $43.91.

This makes it the sixth lowest performer in the NASDAQ-100 Index which was up 5.5% in the past week; a relative price change of -11.1%.

During the week its volatility (highest price minus lowest price/lowest price) of 6.9% was 2 times its average weekly volatility of 4.1%.

In the past week the market cap has declined by $2.6 billion to $44.3 billion.

The volume was 1.2 times average trading per week of 35.7 million shares.

Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ:EXC) is a leading utility company centered on electricity generation and distribution, supporting resilient infrastructure and sustainable energy investments through modernized smart grid solutions. Integrating advanced technologies, Exelon's ComEd electrification strategy aims to manage a 19 GW load increase by 2030 within Illinois by leveraging Exelon virtual power plant services and demand response capabilities. The company's innovation pipeline includes pioneering the Exelon V2G pilot program and distributed energy resources integration, enhancing both grid services and capacity planning. Strategic capital expenditure plans underscore Exelon’s competitive strengths in renewable energy integration and utility modernization efforts.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

Exelon Corporation is a prominent U.S.-based energy company listed on the Nasdaq, specializing in the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity, as well as natural gas delivery. As one of the largest utilities in the nation, it operates through subsidiaries like Commonwealth Edison, PECO, and BGE, serving millions of customers across multiple states. The company's core activities encompass a diverse energy portfolio, including nuclear, fossil fuel, and renewable sources, with a strong emphasis on reliable and sustainable power supply. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, particularly wind and solar projects, driven by increasing demand for clean energy and supportive regulatory environments. Exelon is also advancing grid modernization and smart technology investments to enhance efficiency and integrate more renewables, positioning it as a key player in the transition to a low-carbon future while maintaining operational excellence and financial stability. It is the NASDAQ's 4th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ:EXC) is a leading energy provider strategically positioned to capitalize on the clean energy transition. With a robust Exelon growth strategy, the company focuses on Exelon infrastructure investment and Exelon grid modernization, bolstering its status as a key player in power generation. Exelon's comprehensive Exelon capital expenditures and Exelon capex plans underscore its commitment to modernizing its Exelon nuclear fleet and advancing Exelon transmission projects. The firm’s Exelon capital allocation prioritizes Exelon renewable energy projects and Exelon distribution upgrades, enhancing grid reliability. Recent ventures, including Exelon Constellation acquisition, fortify its market position. Exelon's clean energy transition aligns with its sustainability goals and Exelon utility services contribute significantly to earnings guidance. Analyst ratings on Exelon (EXC) stock reflect confidence in Exelon stock outlook and Exelon dividend outlook. As EXC quarterly results continue to meet expectations, Exelon long-term targets remain focused on infrastructure capex and modernization plans.

urn:contentItem:6JHV-2T23-S4GG-D4TY-00000-00Quarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 28 March 2026: Littelfuse reports 10% sequential rise in Quarterly Revenue Corporate Wire Date: 06 May 2026 23:38 EDT Release Date: May 06, 20262026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:48ZLexisNexisQuarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 28 March 2026: Littelfuse reports 10% sequential rise in Quarterly Revenue Corporate Wire Date: 06 May 2026 23:38 EDT Release Date: May 06, 2026

AMERICAN RESULTS

Littelfuse (NASDAQ:LFUS), announced total revenue of $655m for the quarter-ended 28 March 2026 [Q1/2026], up 10% from the previous quarter [Q4/2025] and up 17% from the year-earlier period [Q1/2025].

Major changes compared with previous corresponding period [Mar 28, 2026 vs Mar 29, 2025]:

Favourable Changes:

- Net profit was up 72.5% from $43.6m to $75.1m

- Total revenue was up 17.1% from $559.1m to $654.6m

- EPS growth 70.5% from $1.76 to $3.0

- EBITDA to Sales was up from 14.9% to 18.4%

- EBIT to Sales was up from 12.3% to 15.8%

- Profit before tax to Sales was up from 10.7% to 14.8%

- Profit after tax to Sales was up from 7.8% to 11.5%

Note:

- Tax expenses to Sales was up from 2.9% to 3.3%

Quarterly Report (Quarter ending 28 Mar 2026)

Quarter-ended28 Mar 202627 Dec 202527 Sep 2025EPS, $3.0-9.82.8Revenue, $ million655594625Sequential growth in Revenue %10.2-4.91.8Net Profit, $ million75.1-24269.5

Compared with the Previous Corresponding Period [PCP; Q1/2025], year-over-year [y.o.y.] EPS was up 70.5%, Revenue was up 17.1% and Net Profit was up 72.5%.

Quarter-ended28 Mar 202629 Mar 2025EPS$3.0176cPCP growth in EPS %70-9.7Revenue, $ million655559PCP growth in Revenue %17.14.4Net Profit, $ million75.143.6PCP growth in Net Profit %72-10.1

ACTIVITIES:

Littelfuse, Inc., a global leader in circuit protection technologies, has been providing innovative solutions since its founding in 1927. Headquartered in Chicago, Illinois, the company designs, manufactures, and markets a diverse range of products, including fuses, semiconductors, sensors, and protection relays, primarily serving the automotive, industrial, and electronics sectors. Its core business revolves around safeguarding electrical systems from overcurrent, overvoltage, and other hazards, enhancing safety and reliability across applications. The fastest-growing segments include automotive electronics, fueled by the rising adoption of electric vehicles and advanced driver-assistance systems, as well as industrial automation and renewable energy solutions, where demand for efficient protection components is surging. With a strong international footprint, Littelfuse continues to invest in R&D to address evolving market demands and maintain its competitive edge.

MARKET POSITION:

Littelfuse, Inc. (NASDAQ:LFUS), the NASDAQ's 5th largest Electronics company by market cap, soared $189.31 (or 75%) year-to-date (YTD) in 2026 to close at $441.69. Compared with the NASDAQ-100 Index which has risen 13.3% YTD, this is a relative price increase of 61.7%. A zero-bagger in 2025, the value of USD1,000 invested two years ago is $1,868 [vs $1,594 for the NASDAQ-100 Index], for a capital gain of $826 and dividend reinvested of $42. The average annualised return to shareholders has been 36.7% in the past two years. The average daily volume of 236,584 shares so far in 2026 was 1.2 times the average daily volume in 2025.

urn:contentItem:6JJ7-CNB3-S90S-81PF-00000-00Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ:EXC) falls 5.6% in Week 18 of 2026: SEC Filings, Insider Filings and Financial Highlights2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:48ZLexisNexisExelon Corporation (NASDAQ:EXC) falls 5.6% in Week 18 of 2026: SEC Filings, Insider Filings and Financial Highlights

NASDAQ END-OF-WEEK STOCK REPORT

www.buysellsignals.com

Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ:EXC $43.91), the NASDAQ's 4th largest Utility company by market cap, has fell $2.59 (or 5.6%) in the past week to close at $43.91.

This makes it the sixth lowest performer in the NASDAQ-100 Index which was up 5.5% in the past week; a relative price change of -11.1%.

During the week its volatility (highest price minus lowest price/lowest price) of 6.9% was 2 times its average weekly volatility of 4.1%.

In the past week the market cap has declined by $2.6 billion to $44.3 billion.

The volume was 1.2 times average trading per week of 35.7 million shares.

Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ:EXC) is a leading utility company centered on electricity generation and distribution, supporting resilient infrastructure and sustainable energy investments through modernized smart grid solutions. Integrating advanced technologies, Exelon's ComEd electrification strategy aims to manage a 19 GW load increase by 2030 within Illinois by leveraging Exelon virtual power plant services and demand response capabilities. The company's innovation pipeline includes pioneering the Exelon V2G pilot program and distributed energy resources integration, enhancing both grid services and capacity planning. Strategic capital expenditure plans underscore Exelon’s competitive strengths in renewable energy integration and utility modernization efforts.

Financial News

Annual Report (FY 2025):

Release Date: February 13, 2026

FY2025 Annual Report: Exelon Net Profit up 13%

Exelon (NASDAQ:EXC) reported net profit for the year-ended 31 December 2025 [FY2025] of $2.8b, up 13% from $2.5b in the previous year [FY2024]. Earnings per share (EPS) were up 12% from $2.45 in FY2024 to $2.74 in FY2025.

Annual growth in Revenue, Net Profit and EPS

Friday's price drop

Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ:EXC) in 4th consecutive fall

CHICAGO, Friday, May 08 2026 - Exelon Corporation's (NASDAQ: EXC $43.91) stock price slipped further on Friday, sliding an additional 50.0c (1.1%) to close at $43.91. There were 5,422,813 shares worth $238.1 million traded today.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

Exelon Corporation is a prominent U.S.-based energy company listed on the Nasdaq, specializing in the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity, as well as natural gas delivery. As one of the largest utilities in the nation, it operates through subsidiaries like Commonwealth Edison, PECO, and BGE, serving millions of customers across multiple states. The company's core activities encompass a diverse energy portfolio, including nuclear, fossil fuel, and renewable sources, with a strong emphasis on reliable and sustainable power supply. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, particularly wind and solar projects, driven by increasing demand for clean energy and supportive regulatory environments. Exelon is also advancing grid modernization and smart technology investments to enhance efficiency and integrate more renewables, positioning it as a key player in the transition to a low-carbon future while maintaining operational excellence and financial stability. It is the NASDAQ's 4th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ:EXC) is a leading energy provider strategically positioned to capitalize on the clean energy transition. With a robust Exelon growth strategy, the company focuses on Exelon infrastructure investment and Exelon grid modernization, bolstering its status as a key player in power generation. Exelon's comprehensive Exelon capital expenditures and Exelon capex plans underscore its commitment to modernizing its Exelon nuclear fleet and advancing Exelon transmission projects. The firm’s Exelon capital allocation prioritizes Exelon renewable energy projects and Exelon distribution upgrades, enhancing grid reliability. Recent ventures, including Exelon Constellation acquisition, fortify its market position. Exelon's clean energy transition aligns with its sustainability goals and Exelon utility services contribute significantly to earnings guidance. Analyst ratings on Exelon (EXC) stock reflect confidence in Exelon stock outlook and Exelon dividend outlook. As EXC quarterly results continue to meet expectations, Exelon long-term targets remain focused on infrastructure capex and modernization plans.

value.Title: May 02, 2026: Virgin Media O2 Expands Renewable Capacity with egg Power Solar PPA, Baker McKenzie Leads

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHR-F9P3-SCRX-62XB-00000-00May 02, 2026: Virgin Media O2 Expands Renewable Capacity with egg Power Solar PPA, Baker McKenzie Leads2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:48ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSMay 02, 2026: Virgin Media O2 Expands Renewable Capacity with egg Power Solar PPA, Baker McKenzie Leads

Baker McKenzie has advised Virgin Media O2 on its latest long-term Power Purchase Agreement (PPA) with egg Power, formalized on the 5th of May 2026. The deal reinforces Virgin Media O2's ongoing push toward decarbonised energy.

Under the 10-year term, egg Power will deliver solar energy from its forthcoming Suffolk solar farm, expected to start operations in 2027.

The solar allocation accounts for about 5% of Virgin Media O2's total energy requirements, while the move sits alongside a prior wind energy arrangement with The Renewables Infrastructure Group in 2025, also facilitated by Baker McKenzie.

Together, the wind and solar PPAs are expected to meet around 20% of the company's total energy demand as part of its decarbonisation drive.

The Baker McKenzie team supporting the deal was led by Energy & Infrastructure Partner James Wyatt, with Associate Alex Tam, and contributions from Consultant Andrew Hedges and Trainee Kayan Sayeed. Wyatt commented, 'This agreement represents a further step in Virgin Media O2's transition toward sustainable energy procurement, and we were pleased to support our client on another strategically important PPA, reflecting our continued focus on advising on complex energy and decarbonisation driven projects.'

As companies increasingly prioritise sustainability, this partnership exemplifies the corporate sector's ongoing shift toward renewable energy.

INDEX

SECTION 1 BAKER MCKENZIE PROFILE

SECTION 2 BAKER MCKENZIE TOP MANAGEMENT

SECTION 1 BAKER MCKENZIE PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

BakerMcKenzie, founded in 1949, is a global law firm headquartered in Chicago, Illinois. With over 6,000 lawyers across 77 offices in 46 countries, it serves clients in energy, technology, and finance, specializing in tax, M&A, and dispute resolution. Generating over $3 billion annually, its a leader in cross-border transactions, advising on global ESG compliance. In 2024, Baker McKenzie enhanced its AI and digital transformation practices, per its website. Its culture emphasizes inclusion, collaboration, and pro bono work, with a top ranking in employee benefits by Legal 500. Competing with DLA Piper, Baker McKenzie stands out for its global reach and local expertise. Recent initiatives include sustainable finance advisory and cloud-based legal tech, aligning with market trends. The Baker & McKenzie Foundation supports community programs, reinforcing its social commitment.

Vision and Mission

Vision

To be acknowledged as the most trusted global law firm, empowering clients to achieve their business goals and spearhead innovation.

Mission

Baker McKenzie aims to help clients traverse the complexities of an evolving world by offering a unique mix of local insights and global context.

Core Values

Bravery -Baker McKenzie embraces courage and innovation, working collaboratively to overcome industry obstacles.

Client Focus -Putting clients first, the firm is dedicated to delivering service excellence.

Inclusion and Diversity -Valuing individuality, Baker McKenzie fosters a culture of empowerment and growth.

Integrity -Conducting business with honesty and transparency, Baker McKenzie builds trust with clients and partners.

Global Perspective -The firm provides a connected approach to business challenges, transcending traditional lines of practice and markets.

urn:contentItem:6JHK-KSM3-S0RF-V4B5-00000-00Quarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 28 March 2026: Littelfuse reports 10% sequential rise in Quarterly Revenue Corporate Wire Date: 06 May 2026 23:38 EDT Release Date: May 06, 20262026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:48ZLexisNexisQuarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 28 March 2026: Littelfuse reports 10% sequential rise in Quarterly Revenue Corporate Wire Date: 06 May 2026 23:38 EDT Release Date: May 06, 2026

AMERICAN RESULTS

Littelfuse (NASDAQ:LFUS), announced total revenue of $655m for the quarter-ended 28 March 2026 [Q1/2026], up 10% from the previous quarter [Q4/2025] and up 17% from the year-earlier period [Q1/2025].

Major changes compared with previous corresponding period [Mar 28, 2026 vs Mar 29, 2025]:

Favourable Changes:

- Net profit was up 72.5% from $43.6m to $75.1m

- Total revenue was up 17.1% from $559.1m to $654.6m

- EPS growth 70.5% from $1.76 to $3.0

- EBITDA to Sales was up from 14.9% to 18.4%

- EBIT to Sales was up from 12.3% to 15.8%

- Profit before tax to Sales was up from 10.7% to 14.8%

- Profit after tax to Sales was up from 7.8% to 11.5%

Note:

- Tax expenses to Sales was up from 2.9% to 3.3%

Quarterly Report (Quarter ending 28 Mar 2026)

Quarter-ended28 Mar 202627 Dec 202527 Sep 2025EPS, $3.0-9.82.8Revenue, $ million655594625Sequential growth in Revenue %10.2-4.91.8Net Profit, $ million75.1-24269.5

Compared with the Previous Corresponding Period [PCP; Q1/2025], year-over-year [y.o.y.] EPS was up 70.5%, Revenue was up 17.1% and Net Profit was up 72.5%.

Quarter-ended28 Mar 202629 Mar 2025EPS$3.0176cPCP growth in EPS %70-9.7Revenue, $ million655559PCP growth in Revenue %17.14.4Net Profit, $ million75.143.6PCP growth in Net Profit %72-10.1

ACTIVITIES:

Littelfuse, Inc., a global leader in circuit protection technologies, has been providing innovative solutions since its founding in 1927. Headquartered in Chicago, Illinois, the company designs, manufactures, and markets a diverse range of products, including fuses, semiconductors, sensors, and protection relays, primarily serving the automotive, industrial, and electronics sectors. Its core business revolves around safeguarding electrical systems from overcurrent, overvoltage, and other hazards, enhancing safety and reliability across applications. The fastest-growing segments include automotive electronics, fueled by the rising adoption of electric vehicles and advanced driver-assistance systems, as well as industrial automation and renewable energy solutions, where demand for efficient protection components is surging. With a strong international footprint, Littelfuse continues to invest in R&D to address evolving market demands and maintain its competitive edge.

MARKET POSITION:

Littelfuse, Inc. (NASDAQ:LFUS), the NASDAQ's 5th largest Electronics company by market cap, soared $189.31 (or 75%) year-to-date (YTD) in 2026 to close at $441.69. Compared with the NASDAQ-100 Index which has risen 13.3% YTD, this is a relative price increase of 61.7%. A zero-bagger in 2025, the value of USD1,000 invested two years ago is $1,868 [vs $1,594 for the NASDAQ-100 Index], for a capital gain of $826 and dividend reinvested of $42. The average annualised return to shareholders has been 36.7% in the past two years. The average daily volume of 236,584 shares so far in 2026 was 1.2 times the average daily volume in 2025.

value.Title: May 02, 2026: Baker McKenzie Advises Virgin Media O2 on 10-Year PPA with egg Power

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHM-KD23-RXGW-R1TF-00000-00May 02, 2026: Baker McKenzie Advises Virgin Media O2 on 10-Year PPA with egg Power2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:48ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSMay 02, 2026: Baker McKenzie Advises Virgin Media O2 on 10-Year PPA with egg Power

Baker McKenzie has advised Virgin Media O2 on its latest long-term Power Purchase Agreement (PPA) with egg Power. The agreement, formalized on the 5th of May 2026, marks a significant step in Virgin Media O2's commitment to sustainable energy solutions.

Under the 10-year term, egg Power will deliver solar energy from its forthcoming solar farm in Suffolk, due to begin operations in 2027. The renewable energy is projected to account for about 5% of Virgin Media O2's total energy requirements.

This PPA builds on Virgin Media O2's broader clean energy strategy, which also includes a prior wind energy arrangement with The Renewables Infrastructure Group in 2025, facilitated by Baker McKenzie. Together, these two deals are expected to meet around 20% of the company’s energy demand, underscoring its decarbonisation push. The Baker McKenzie team was led by Energy & Infrastructure Partner James Wyatt, with Associate Alex Tam, supported by Consultant Andrew Hedges and Trainee Kayan Sayeed. Wyatt said, 'This agreement represents a further step in Virgin Media O2's transition toward sustainable energy procurement, and we were pleased to support our client on another strategically important PPA, reflecting our continued focus on advising on complex energy and decarbonisation driven projects.'

INDEX

SECTION 1 BAKER MCKENZIE PROFILE

SECTION 2 BAKER MCKENZIE TOP MANAGEMENT

SECTION 1 BAKER MCKENZIE PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

BakerMcKenzie, founded in 1949, is a global law firm headquartered in Chicago, Illinois. With over 6,000 lawyers across 77 offices in 46 countries, it serves clients in energy, technology, and finance, specializing in tax, M&A, and dispute resolution. Generating over $3 billion annually, its a leader in cross-border transactions, advising on global ESG compliance. In 2024, Baker McKenzie enhanced its AI and digital transformation practices, per its website. Its culture emphasizes inclusion, collaboration, and pro bono work, with a top ranking in employee benefits by Legal 500. Competing with DLA Piper, Baker McKenzie stands out for its global reach and local expertise. Recent initiatives include sustainable finance advisory and cloud-based legal tech, aligning with market trends. The Baker & McKenzie Foundation supports community programs, reinforcing its social commitment.

Vision and Mission

Vision

To be acknowledged as the most trusted global law firm, empowering clients to achieve their business goals and spearhead innovation.

Mission

Baker McKenzie aims to help clients traverse the complexities of an evolving world by offering a unique mix of local insights and global context.

Core Values

Bravery -Baker McKenzie embraces courage and innovation, working collaboratively to overcome industry obstacles.

Client Focus -Putting clients first, the firm is dedicated to delivering service excellence.

Inclusion and Diversity -Valuing individuality, Baker McKenzie fosters a culture of empowerment and growth.

Integrity -Conducting business with honesty and transparency, Baker McKenzie builds trust with clients and partners.

Global Perspective -The firm provides a connected approach to business challenges, transcending traditional lines of practice and markets.

value.Title: Silgan Closures Invests in Cleaner Energy in Illinois

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH6-92J3-SCHS-X0FH-00000-00Silgan Closures Invests in Cleaner Energy in Illinois2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:48ZLexisNexisSilgan Closures Invests in Cleaner Energy in IllinoisDOWNERS GROVE, Ill., May 5, 2026

PR NewswireGlobal manufacturer invests to achieve Illinois' renewable energy requirementsDOWNERS GROVE, Ill., May 5, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Silgan Closures, a global leader in food and beverage closure manufacturing with headquarters in Illinois, is joining statewide efforts to expand energy reliability and sustainability by investing in community wind and solar projects. The Company's goal is to offset 75 percent of its electricity consumption with clean energy credits within the next five years and to reduce its own energy use at two manufacturing facilities in Illinois.

Illinois legislation presently calls for all electricity in the state to come from renewable sources and nuclear power by 2050. To help meet this goal, Silgan Closures has partnered with leading renewable energy developers to contractually support wind and solar electricity generation projects in Illinois."At Silgan Closures, our roots go back nearly a century to a family-owned Illinois business. Today, as a global leader in food and beverage closure manufacturing, we remain deeply embedded in the communities we serve," said Silgan Closures President Liz Wiese. "Our latest investments in cleaner energy here in Illinois reflect that commitment – not only to our customers, but to driving sustainable economic growth where our employees live, work, and rely on our products in their homes."Additionally, through its partnership with the U.S. Department of Energy's Better Plants Program, Silgan Closures is taking steps to increase energy efficiency at its facilities in Illinois, which is expected to result in reduced energy consumption at these plants.About Silgan Closures:Silgan Closures is a global supplier of plastic, metal, and composite closure systems for food and beverage products. The Silgan Closures U.S. headquarters and innovation center are located in Downers Grove, Illinois. The European headquarters are located in Munich, Germany with global operations across five continents. For more information, visit the company website atwww.silgancls.comor contact Theadora Iacobellis at .
